channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Experimental drug shows promise in reversing fatty liver disease through gut repair
item.description: An experimental drug called DT-109 has demonstrated the ability to reverse severe fatty liver disease (MASH) in animal studies by repairing the gut and preventing harmful toxins from damaging the live...

item.title: U.S. Launches Third Round of Strikes Against Iran Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: The United States has launched a third round of strikes against Iran, marking the latest escalation in regional tensions. U.S. Central Command confirmed the attacks began around 7:30 ET on Saturday, r...

item.title: Two Arrested for Arson in Manipur Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Two individuals were arrested for allegedly setting fire to six abandoned houses in Manipur's Kanto Sabal village, sparking tensions between locals and security forces. The incident occurred on July 1...

item.title: Guide on accessing and printing KCSE results via KNEC portal
item.description: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to access and print the KCSE result slip through the KNEC portal. It outlines the steps to log in using your 11-digit index number and registered nam...

item.title: The Evolutionary Role of the Uvula in Human Speech and Anatomy
item.description: The uvula, a small tissue flap hanging from the soft palate, is a unique feature among mammals and has sparked debate among biologists about its evolutionary purpose. While some argue it evolved to su...

item.title: Ben&Ben's heartfelt rendition of 'Harana' at OPM Con with Chito Miranda and Flow G's reimagining
item.description: The recent OPM Con event showcased a heartfelt performance of the classic song 'Harana' by Ben&Ben, accompanied by collaborations from artists like Chito Miranda and Flow G. The concert, held on July ...

item.title: FIFA confirms Jude Bellingham’s goal against Norway was valid after technical review
item.description: FIFA has clarified that England’s first goal against Norway in the 2026 World Cup quarter-final was valid after reviewing ball-tracking data. Jude Bellingham’s early goal helped England draw level wit...

item.title: Ferrari's Charles Leclerc claims British Grand Prix victory
item.description: Ferrari's Charles Leclerc secured a dominant victory at the British Grand Prix, showcasing his skill and the team's competitiveness. The race, held at the Silverstone Circuit, saw Leclerc maintain a s...

item.title: VAR's Role in Egypt-Argentina World Cup Clash Sparks Debate
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding Video Assistant Referee (VAR) decisions during the Egypt-Argentina World Cup match. VAR, introduced to minimize errors in critical moments, faced back...

item.title: Kate Hudson Shares Family Vacation in Greece and Reflections on Raising Children
item.description: The article discusses Kate Hudson's recent family vacation in Greece, where she shared photos of herself wearing various bikini outfits. While the initial focus is on her appearance, the piece also de...

item.title: DOJ's vague response sparks confusion over Epstein investigation delays
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) faced criticism for its ambiguous response to allegations that the Trump administration was hindering an active investigation into Jeffrey Epstein's New Mexico pro...

item.title: Experts Urge Comprehensive Strategies to Tackle Rising Heart Disease Rates
item.description: A leading health expert, Professor Mkumbo, has warned that current efforts to combat cardiovascular diseases are inadequate, urging stronger measures to address the growing health crisis. The article ...

item.title: Environment Canada Issues Tornado Warning for Parts of Manitoba
item.description: Environment Canada has issued a tornado warning for parts of Manitoba, indicating that a severe thunderstorm may be producing a tornado. As of 4:03 p.m. CDT on Saturday, meteorologists are tracking a ...

item.title: How Declan Rice and Bukayo Saka's Partners Are Redefining Support Squad Dynamics in Football
item.description: The article explores the evolving role of footballers' partners in modern sports culture, focusing on high-profile players like Declan Rice and Bukayo Saka. It highlights how their spouses—such as E D...

item.title: England and Norway Tie in Thrilling World Cup Quarterfinal Match
item.description: The World Cup quarterfinal clash between England and Norway delivered an intense and controversial encounter that ended in a 1-1 draw, forcing extra time. Norway took an early lead in the 36th minute ...

item.title: Abia Police Arrest 22-Year-Old Suspect Using Lighter as Weapon
item.description: A 22-year-old man in Abia State was arrested by police after using a stainless lighter, designed to look like a pistol, to rob members of the public. The suspect, from Obinolu Ngodo Isiochi, was appre...

item.title: Addressing Bureaucratic Abuse in Public Service Reforms
item.description: The article critiques the recent amendment to South Africa's Public Service Act, which aims to depoliticise appointments by transferring powers to heads of departments. While the intention to combat c...

item.title: Fifteen Indian tourists killed and 17 injured after speedboat capsizes near Vietnam's Phu Quoc island
item.description: At least 15 Indian tourists lost their lives and 17 others were injured after a speedboat carrying 32 Indian passengers and four crew members capsized around 400-500 metres from Hon May Rut Ngoai isla...

item.title: Exploring the Meaning of 'Gratefully' in Modern Usage
item.description: The article delves into the word 'gratefully,' examining its evolving meaning and usage in contemporary language. Fr. Raymund Benedict Hizon, SJ, discusses how the term has become undefined in modern ...

item.title: Peter Obi Rejects Debate with Umahi, Demands He Run for Presidency First
item.description: Peter Obi, the 2027 presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), has refused to engage in a debate with Minister of Works Dave Umahi. During an interview with Jude Chidenowo, Obi s...
